title: Systems for thrombectomy
abstract: The disclosure provides medical systems, kits and methods for retrieval and/or extraction of a corpus located in a tubular organ. Systems of this disclosure are suitable for carrying out various procedures for removal of occlusive corpus from tubular organs, for example thrombectomy.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A medical system for anchoring into at least one corpus located in tubular organ, the system comprising a handling and manipulation apparatus (HMA) and a corpus anchoring unit operable thereby, the HMA being configured for manipulating the corpus anchoring unit into engagement with said corpus; the corpus anchoring unit comprising: a deployment wire defining a proximal-distal axis, at least two generally cylindrical elongated bodies that are spaced apart along said deployment wire, each of said bodies having a proximal end and a distal end and being constituted by at least one wound coiled thread in its deployment state, each of said bodies having a fixed end at either the proximal or distal end and having a free, opposite end that is configured for deploying into at least one deployed state in which each of the threads unwinds in the general radial direction while tracing, during deployment, a generally helical path, and at least two axially displaceable tip tools, each mounted onto the deployment wire and associated with the free end of the bodies such that axial displacement of the tip tool forces the wound coiled threads to unwind into the at least one deployed state; the HMA being configured to axially displace the deployment wire. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein each of said bodies being configured to unwind at a force applied thereto upon axial displacement of the tip tool, which may be the same or different than that applied onto other bodies. 3. The system of claim 1 or 2, wherein the number of the bodies is between 2 and 10. 4. The system of any one of claims 1-3, wherein said bodies are arranged such that (i) odd bodies having free ends at respective distal ends, while even bodies having free ends at respective proximal ends, or (ii) odd bodies having free ends at respective proximal ends, while even bodies having free ends at respective distal ends. 5. The system of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein at least one of the bodies is fixed to said deployment wire. 6. The system of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein at least one of the bodies is floating. 7. The system of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein at least one of the tip tools is fixed to said deployment wire. 8. The system of claim 7, wherein at least one other of the tip tools is floating. 9. The system of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein (i) bodies having a distal free end are fixed to said deployment wire and bodies having a proximal free end are floating, or (ii) bodies having a proximal free end are fixed to said deployment wire and bodies having a distal free end are floating. 10. The system of claim 9, wherein fixed bodies are associated with floating tip tools, and floating bodies are associated with fixed tip tools. 11. The system of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein (i) the threads of at least one of the bodies are coiled to permit helical unwinding movement in one rotational direction upon axial displacement of its corresponding tip tool and (ii) the threads of a consecutive body along the proximal-distal axis are coiled to permit their helical unwinding movement in an opposite rotational direction upon axial displacement of its corresponding tip tool. 12. A medical system for anchoring into at least one corpus located in a tubular organ, the system comprising a handling and manipulation apparatus (HMA) and a corpus anchoring unit operable thereby, the HMA being configured for manipulating the corpus anchoring unit into engagement with said corpus, the corpus anchoring unit comprising: a deployment wire defining a proximal-distal axis; at least one pair of generally cylindrical elongated bodies spaced apart along said deployment wire, each of said bodies having a proximal end and a distal end, and being constituted by at least one wound coiled thread in its deployment state, a first of the pair of bodies being a proximal body with a proximal fixed end and a distal free end and a second of the pair of bodies being a distal body with a distal fixed end and a proximal free end, the free end of each body being configured for deploying into a deployed state in which each of the threads unwinds in the general radial direction while tracing, during deployment, a generally helical path, and 

 at least one pair of axially displaceable tip tools, each mounted onto the deployment wire an being associated with the body's free end, such that axial displacement of the tip tool forces the wound coiled threads of each of the pair of bodies to unwind into said at least one deployed state, the HMA being configured to axially displace the deployment wire. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein unwinding of the coiled threads of said pair of bodies causes entanglement of the unwound threads of one of the bodies in the deployed state into the unwound threads of the other body of said pair in the deployed state. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein the unwound threads of said pair of bodies form a cage structure. 15. The system of any one of claims 12 to 14, comprising at least 2 pairs of bodies and at least 2 pairs of tip tools associated therewith. 16. The system of any one of claims 12 to 15, wherein the length of the first body of said pair of bodies is larger than the length of the second body of said pair of bodies. 17. The system of any one of claims 12 to 16, wherein the system further comprises at least one additional generally cylindrical elongated body, spaced apart on said deployment wire from said at least one pair of bodies, said additional body being associated with an additional tip tool at a proximal end or a distal end of said additional body. 18. The system of any one of claims 12 to 17, wherein (i) the threads of the proximal body of the pair of bodies are coiled to permit helical unwinding movement in one rotational direction upon axial displacement of its corresponding tip tool and (ii) the threads of the distal body of said pair of bodies are coiled to permit their helical unwinding movement in an opposite rotational direction upon axial displacement of its corresponding tip tool. 19. The system of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein said bodies are spaced apart by a spacer. 20. The system of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein each body, independently of the others, comprises between 1 and 120 wound coiled threads. 21. The system of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the tip tool is configured to simultaneously unwind all of the wound coiled threads in its associated body. 22. The system of any one of claims 1 to 21, wherein said wound coiled threads are made of a shape-memory metal or alloy. 23. The system of any one of claims 1 to 22, wherein the threads are dimensioned to exert a radial force of no more than IN (in a conduit having a diameter of 2 mm) on an internal surface of the tubular anatomical organ. 24. The system of any one of claims 1 to 23, further comprising at least one closed tube. 25. The system of any one of claims 1 to 24, wherein said tip tool having an ellipsoid shape, such that the longitudinal axis of the tip tool coincides with the deployment wire. 26. The system of claim 25, wherein the maximal diameter of the tip tool is larger than the internal diameter of the associated body. 27. The system of claim 25 or 26, wherein each tip tool comprises a tubular element associated with one of the tip tool's proximal or distal ends. 28. The system of claim 27, wherein (i) when the tip tool is associated with a proximal end of a corresponding body, the tubular element is at a proximal end of the tip tool, or (ii) when the tip tool is associated with a distal end of a corresponding body, the tubular element is at a distal end of the tip tool. 29. The system of any one of claims 1 to 28, wherein at least one of the tip tools comprises an radiopaque marker, e.g. a platinum iridium (Pt-Ir) or gold marker. 30. The system of any one of claims 1 to 29, wherein the external surface of at least one of the bodies is enveloped by a polymeric layer along a portion of the body's length. 31. The system of claim 30, wherein said polymeric layer is made of a polymeric material selected from polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE), polyethylene, polypropylene or polyurethanes. 32. The system of claim 30 or 31 , wherein said polymeric layer limits the extent of unwinding of the coiled threads. 33. The system of any one of claims 1 to 32, further comprising at least one embolic protection element. 34. The system of claim 33, wherein said embolic protection element being positioned either at the proximal end or at the distal end of the corpus anchoring unit. 35. The system of claim 33 or 34, wherein said embolic protection element is displaceable along the deployment wire to cover the corpus anchoring unit. 36. The system of claim 35, wherein said embolic protection element is an avertable sheet. 37. The system of any one of claims 34 to 36, wherein said embolic protection element is an occlusion balloon. 38. The system of any one of claims 1 to 37, wherein the tubular organ is selected from a blood vessel, fallopian tubes, urinary tract, ureter, urethra, biliary tract, bile ducts, gastrointestinal tract, airways and any other anatomical lumen. 39. The system of any one of claims 1 to 38, wherein each of the cylindrical bodies and/or tip tools may, independently, comprise a substance to be eluted over time. 40. A kit for assembly of the system of any one of claims 1 to 39, the kit comprising a handling and manipulation apparatus (HMA), at least one deployment wire; a plurality of generally cylindrical elongated bodies, each body being constituted by at least one shape-memory metal or alloy wound coiled thread; and a plurality of tip tools; optionally comprising a plurality of spacers and/or an embolic protection element. 41. The kit of claim 40, further comprising means for associating the deployment wire with (i) the HMA, (ii) the bodies, and/or (iii) the tip tools. 42. A kit comprising the system of any one of claims 1 to 39 and instructions for use. 43. A method for removal of a corpus located in a tubular anatomical organ, comprising: (a) manipulating a corpus anchoring unit by a handling and manipulation apparatus (HMA) associated therewith, such that the corpus anchoring unit is brought into proximity with the corpus, the corpus anchoring unit comprising: a deployment wire defining a proximal-distal axis; at least two generally cylindrical elongated bodies that are spaced apart along said deployment wire, each body having a proximal end and a distal end and being constituted by at least one wound coiled thread in a 

 deployment state, each of said bodies having a fixed end at either the proximal or distal end and having a free, opposite end that is configured for deploying into at least one deployed state in which each of the threads unwinds in the general radial direction while tracing, during deployment, a generally helical path and at least two axially displaceable tip tools, each mounted onto the deployment wire and associated with the free end of the bodies; (b) axially displacing the deployment wire to axially displace at least one tip tool, thereby unwinding at least one coiled thread from its associated body from the deployment state to said at least one deployed state , thereby anchoring the unwound coiled thread into the corpus; and (c) removing the anchored corpus from the organ by manipulating the corpus anchoring unit out of the organ. 44. The method of claim 43, wherein each of said bodies being configured to unwind at a force applied thereto upon axial displacement of the tip tool, which may be the same or different than that applied onto other bodies. 45. A method for removal of a corpus located in a tubular anatomical organ, comprising: (a) manipulating a corpus anchoring unit by a handling and manipulation apparatus (HMA) associated therewith, such that the corpus anchoring unit is brought into proximity with the corpus, the corpus anchoring unit comprising: a deployment wire defining a proximal-distal axis; at least one pair of generally cylindrical elongated bodies that are spaced apart along said deployment wire, each body having a proximal end and a distal end and being constituted by at least one wound coiled thread in a deployment state, a first of the pair of bodies being a proximal body with a proximal fixed end and a distal free end and a second of the pair of bodies being a distal body with a distal fixed end and a proximal free end, the free end of each body being configured for deploying into a deployed state in which each of the threads unwinds in the general radial direction while tracing, during deployment, a generally helical path,, and 

 at least one pair of axially displaceable tip tools each mounted onto the deployment wire, a first of said pair of tip tools being associated with the distal free end of the first of the pair of bodies, and a second of said pair of tip tools being associated with the proximal free end of the second of the pair of bodies, (b) axially displacing the deployment wire in the proximal direction to axially displace the first tip tool in the proximal direction, thereby unwinding at least one coiled thread from the first body from its deployment state to at least one deployed state, thereby anchoring the unwound coiled thread into the corpus; (c) axially displacing the deployment wire in the proximal direction to axially displace the second tip tool in the proximal direction, thereby unwinding at least one coiled thread from the second body from its deployment state to at least one deployed state, thereby anchoring the unwound coiled thread into the corpus; and (d) removing the anchored corpus from the organ by manipulating the corpus anchoring unit out of the organ. 46. The method of claim 45, wherein the system comprises 2 or more pairs of bodies and steps (b)-(c) are repeated for each such pair. 47. The method of claim 45 or 46, further comprises a step (c') between steps (c) and (d): (c') axially displacing the deployment wire to bring the second deployed body into proximity with the first deployed body, thereby forming a cage structure by entangling the unwound coiled threads of the second body and the unwound coiled threads of the first body. 48. The method of claim 47, further comprising a step (c") between step (c') and (d), comprising (c") axially displacing the deployment wire to bring two adjacent cage structures into proximity with one another. 49. A system of any one of claims 1 to 39 for use in removing a corpus from a tubular organ. 50. The system of claim 49, wherein the tubular organ is selected from a blood vessel, fallopian tubes, urinary tract, ureter, urethra, biliary tract, bile ducts, gastrointestinal tract, airways and any other anatomical lumen.
